title: LSC and Massively Documented LLM Hallucination: Prompt Archive, Simulation Lab, and Model-Lineage Extension
description: A publication-grade working paper proposing Massively Documented LLM Hallucination (MDLH) as a formal epistemic-risk framework for AI-assisted scientific discovery, using the LSC neutrino research line as a dual-interpretation case study. The work does not claim that LSC is validated physics or that it is false; it separates unvalidated physics from AI-generated epistemic artifacts. On 2026-04-30 the 6.2.2 correction layer was added to the underlying LSC documentation as a DOI-preserving extension note, and the MHLM archive recorded that repair path explicitly. On 2026-05-05 the public package was extended with a curated model-lineage archive including prompt provenance, dated LLM iteration snapshots, model-specific theory tracks, an experimental simulation lab, and grant-facing audit materials. The 2026-05-05 extension also adds a dated `iterations/LSC_theory_by_LLM/` archive that preserves the final views of Codex, DeepSeek, Gemini, GPT, Kimi, Manus, and other model slots. Public note: these additions improve provenance and hallucination-audit transparency; they do not upgrade the scientific validation status of LSC.
